examples.title: Algorithm: Two-Level Page Table Lookup
examples.description: Hierarchical address translation using outer and inner page tables
examples.code:
ALGORITHM TwoLevelPageLookup
BEGIN
 Extract outer_page from virtual_address
 Extract inner_page from virtual_address
 Extract offset from virtual_address
 
 IF outer_table[outer_page] is invalid THEN
 Raise Page Fault
 END IF
 
 inner_table = outer_table[outer_page].frame
 IF inner_table[inner_page] is invalid THEN
 Raise Page Fault
 END IF
 
 physical_address = (inner_table[inner_page].frame << OFFSET_BITS) | offset
 RETURN physical_address
END
examples.explanation:
1. Splits virtual address into three parts
2. Checks validity at each level
3. Combines frame number with offset

examples.title: Algorithm: Hashed Page Table Lookup
examples.description: Collision resolution using chaining in hashed page tables
examples.code:
ALGORITHM HashedPageLookup
BEGIN
 hash = HASH_FUNCTION(virtual_page_number)
 current = hash_table[hash]
 
 WHILE current != NULL DO
 IF current.pid == pid AND current.vpn == virtual_page_number THEN
 RETURN current.frame
 END IF
 current = current.next
 END WHILE
 
 Raise Page Fault
END
examples.explanation:
1. Computes hash of virtual page number
2. Traverses chain to find matching PID/VPN
3. Returns frame or page fault
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m = number of entries

examples.title: Algorithm: Inverted Page Table Search
examples.description: Linear search through global inverted page table
examples.code:
ALGORITHM InvertedTableSearch
BEGIN
 FOR each entry in inverted_table DO
 IF entry.pid == target_pid AND entry.vpn == target_vpn THEN
 RETURN entry.frame
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 Raise Page Fault
END
examples.explanation:
1. Iterates through all entries
2. Matches PID and virtual page number
3. Returns frame or page fault
